history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed after direct Spring Boot 2.3 -> 3.3 upgrade due to Spring Batch 5 API changes and type incompatibilities in application code.
history.qwen_rationale: Jumping straight from SB 2.3 to 3.3 causes high regression risk. Following empirical best practices, I will first upgrade to SB 2.7 under JDK 11 to stabilize the build, then bump to Java 17, and finally proceed to SB 3.3. This stepwise approach reduces the blast radius of framework API changes and allows intermediate compilation checks.

history.qwen_observation: Build failed at upgrade_spring_boot_3_3 due to type mismatches (LocalDateTime vs Date) and Spring Batch API signature changes, likely caused by running UpgradeBuildToJava17 before the Spring Boot 3 meta-recipe, which disrupts dependency management and API migration order.
history.qwen_rationale: Replacing upgrade_build_to_java17 with upgrade_spring_boot_3_3 aligns with the recommended SB 2.x→3.x migration path. The SB 3 meta-recipe internally handles the Java 17 build configuration and dependency upgrades, ensuring Spring Batch 5 and Spring Framework 6 APIs are applied in the correct order before Java 17 transforms run. This sequencing prevents premature compiler plugin upgrades from conflicting with Spring Boot's managed dependency tree.

history.qwen_rationale: Replacing UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 with UpgradeSpringBoot_3_0 reduces the dependency version jump, potentially avoiding the breaking API changes in Spring Batch and other libraries that caused the compilation errors, while still initiating the necessary Jakarta EE migration for Spring Boot 3.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed at the Spring Boot 3.x upgrade step due to type mismatches (LocalDateTime vs Date) and method signature changes in Spring Batch/Hibernate APIs exposed by the framework bump.
history.qwen_rationale: Replacing the incremental SB 2.7 -> 3.0 path with the recommended UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 meta-recipe aligns with operator guidance for SB 2.x -> 3.x migrations. Adding MigrateToHibernate62 addresses the LocalDateTime/Date type incompatibilities introduced by Hibernate 6 in Spring Boot 3, while the rest of the chain follows the standard JDK 8->21 progression.

history.qwen_observation: Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade fails compilation due to LocalDateTime/Date type mismatches and Spring Batch 5 API signature changes in custom JobControl/DatabaseController classes.
history.qwen_rationale: Inserting MigrateToHibernate62 before the Spring Boot 3 upgrade addresses JPA/Hibernate 6 type changes (LocalDateTime vs Date) and aligns entity mappings early. This pre-conditioning should resolve the type conversion errors, allowing the UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 meta-recipe to successfully apply Spring Batch 5 and other framework migrations without compilation failure.
